verdict_reasoning: TMED9 is a type I transmembrane protein of the p24/TMED family whose canonical and exclusive localization is the early secretory pathway (ER, ERGIC, Golgi). Its luminal GOLD and coiled-coil domains face the endomembrane lumen — not the extracellular space — and its short cytosolic tail binds COPI/COPII coatomers for ER-Golgi retrograde/anterograde cycling. There is no documented plasma membrane pool or outer-leaflet display. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed therapeutic program identified; (2) not an ectodomain-shedding TM precursor — it is an endomembrane-resident cargo receptor with no reported sheddase biology; (3) no wash-resistant TM-partner anchoring to the PM; (4) no documented PM minority pool — cycling is strictly within ER/ERGIC/Golgi compartments; (5) no cell-state-induced (stress, cancer, apoptosis, activation), tissue-restricted, lysosomal-exocytosis, or dual-localization PM pool documented for TMED9 specifically. Verdict: endomembrane-resident.
